Class CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy

java.lang.Object
org.springframework.cloud.circuitbreaker.retry.CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy
public class CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy extends Object
A stateful circuit breaker implementation modeled after Spring Retry's CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy. It maintains state about failures and implements circuit breaker semantics (closed, open, half-open states).

The circuit breaker opens after a single failed retry attempt (all retries exhausted). Once open, requests fail immediately for the duration of the openTimeout. After the openTimeout, the circuit transitions to half-open, allowing one test request. If that succeeds, the circuit closes; if it fails, the circuit reopens.

The resetTimeout determines how long to wait after a failure before resetting the state. If a failure occurs and no more failures happen within the resetTimeout period, the circuit breaker state is reset.

Author:
Ryan Baxter

  • Constructor Details

    • CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy

      public CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy(org.springframework.core.retry.RetryPolicy retryPolicy)
      Create a new circuit breaker retry policy with default settings.
      Parameters:
      retryPolicy - the underlying retry policy to use when the circuit is closed

    • CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy

      public CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy(org.springframework.core.retry.RetryPolicy retryPolicy, Duration openTimeout, Duration resetTimeout)
      Create a new circuit breaker retry policy.
      Parameters:
      retryPolicy - the underlying retry policy to use when the circuit is closed
      openTimeout - the time the circuit stays open before transitioning to half-open
      resetTimeout - the time to wait after a failure before resetting the circuit breaker state

  • Method Details

    • canRetry

      public boolean canRetry()
      Check if a retry should be attempted based on circuit breaker state.
      Returns:
      true if retry should be attempted, false otherwise

    • recordSuccess

      public void recordSuccess()
      Record a successful execution.

    • recordFailure

      public void recordFailure(Throwable exception)
      Record a failed execution. The circuit will open immediately after a single failed execution (after all retries are exhausted).
      Parameters:
      exception - the exception that caused the failure

    • isOpen

      public boolean isOpen()
      Check if the circuit is currently open.
      Returns:
      true if the circuit is open, false otherwise

    • getState

      public CircuitBreakerRetryPolicy.State getState()
      Get the current state of the circuit breaker.
      Returns:
      the current state

    • getLastException

      public @Nullable Throwable getLastException()
      Get the last exception that occurred.
      Returns:
      the last exception, or null if no exception has occurred

    • getRetryPolicy

      public org.springframework.core.retry.RetryPolicy getRetryPolicy()
      Get the underlying retry policy.
      Returns:
      the retry policy

    • getOpenTimeout

      public Duration getOpenTimeout()
      Get the open timeout duration.
      Returns:
      the open timeout

    • getResetTimeout

      public Duration getResetTimeout()
      Get the reset timeout duration.
      Returns:
      the reset timeout

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Reset the circuit breaker to its initial state.
